subject: 3D particle alignement - Head to toe particles
body:
Dear CCPEM community, 

I am faced with a particle alignment problem I can't understand and can't fix. 

The particles are 30S ribosomes. I use RELION-3.1 

A 3D-classification over 8 classes, Tau2 fudge 4 , E-step limit 9Å allowing the coarse sampling option. 
The reference is CTF corrected, initial lowpass filtered at 70Å and reconstructed inside Relion. 

--o Class3D/job282/run --i Select/job281/particles.star 
--ref Refine3D/job137/run_class001.mrc --ini_high 70 
--dont_combine_weights_via_disc --scratch_dir /emscratch --pool 4 --pad 2 --skip_gridding --ctf --ctf_corrected_ref --iter 20 --tau2_fudge 4 --particle_diameter 300 --fast_subsets 
--K 8 --flatten_solvent --zero_mask --strict_highres_exp 9 --oversampling 1 --healpix_order 2 --offset_range 5 --offset_step 2 --allow_coarser_sampling --sym C1 --norm --scale --j 4 --gpu 


In the resulting classes, five were representative of 30S, and all five are the right handedness. 

BUT, one of them was head-to-toe with the others (screenshot 1) and with the reference itself. 

I went back and checked the selected 2D-class averages and saw a similar phenomena (screenshot 2). 

I tried fixing this problem by: 

- re-extracting the particles with re-centering and then running a Refine3D 
- running 3D-classifications with coarser sampling using 1 class or multiple classes. 

This phenomena is recurring. I faced the same problem with three different data sets although sometimes there are multiple head-to-toe classes not just the one (screenshot 3). 



What do you think is the problem, and how can I fix it? 


Ramy KAZAN 
PhD Student, BIOC 
École Polytechnique - CNRS
